word_list.word: a man is as old as he feels himself to be
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈæn ɪz æz ˈoʊld æz hiː fˈiːlz hɪmsˈɛlf tə bˈiː

senses.definition: used to say that a person's age is not solely determined by the number of years they have lived, but by their physical and mental state

word_list.word: an old man is twice a child
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈoʊld mˈæn ɪz twˈaɪs ɐ tʃˈaɪld

senses.definition: used to imply that as a person ages, they become more childlike in their behavior and needs, emphasizing the importance of treating the elderly with care and respect

examples.x: When my grandfather became forgetful and needed constant care, I understood the truth behind the saying an old man is twice a child.

senses.definition: used to suggest that someone who achieves success or matures too quickly or too early in life may also experience a decline or downfall earlier than others

word_list.word: every man is (either|) a fool or physician by (forty|thirty)
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi mˈæn ɪz ˈiːðɚ ɐ fˈuːl ɔːɹ fɪzˈɪʃən baɪ fˈɔːɹɾi θˈɜːɾi

senses.definition: used to imply that as a person reaches middle age, they either gain wisdom and expertise from their experiences or remain foolish due to their inability to learn from mistakes

examples.x: My father always told me that every man is a fool or physician by forty, so I made sure to learn from my mistakes and make better decisions as I approached that milestone age.

senses.definition: used to suggest that the second half of life can be as rewarding as the first, as the age of forty allows more time and resources to focus on personal interests
used to suggest that customs, behavior, and social norms change over time, and what is acceptable or expected in one era or culture may not be in another

word_list.word: there is many a good tune played on an old fiddle
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz mˈɛni ɐ ɡˈʊd tˈuːn plˈeɪd ˌɑːn ɐn ˈoʊld fˈɪdəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that even though something or someone may be old and worn out, they can still be effective and have value
used to imply that young people should be given opportunities to grow, learn, and express themselves while being treated with patience and understanding to nurture their potential

word_list.pronunciation: jˈuːθ wɪl hæv ɪts flˈɪŋ swˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that young people should be allowed to explore and experiment with their lives, make mistakes, and learn from them
